decisionLevelFalsePositives.text: "A trading platform is offering 8% monthly returns on stablecoin deposits. They claim to be registered in Singapore, but I haven't verified the license."
decisionLevelFalsePositives.commentary: The poster is describing an offer and signalling doubt — reporting, not promoting. The classifier keys on the described scam's features; the uncertain-review context patterns do not cover first-person reportage. This is the system's largest real false-positive class, and the reason reviewer confirmation is mandatory before any label (Decision Log 003/004). Candidate fix: a speaker-stance feature (describing vs. soliciting); risk: scammers adopting doubt language as camouflage — see unsolved-002.

decisionLevelFalsePositives.text: "Someone DM'd me about a private crypto investment opportunity. They claim to have insider information about an upcoming token launch, but want a $500 'membership fee.'"

decisionLevelFalsePositives.text: "Can someone please send some BTC 💰 to my crypto wallet? 1KJLqaUxRLpC8UtR8HiQGFj1SmnFNKMCg6"
decisionLevelFalsePositives.commentary: Begging for crypto is not an investment scam under this policy (no promised return, no fake opportunity). Wrong under this policy's definition even if another policy might cover spam or solicitation. Candidate fix: require a return-promise or opportunity claim before transfer-request evidence counts toward labeling.

A short curiosity question tripping free-crypto patterns. Labeling a question as fraud is a category error; at most this deserves a safety resource surface. Candidate fix: interrogative-form dampening.

unsolvedCases.text: ⚠️ Warning: the ETH airdrop links going around are SCAMS. The only real verified claim page is eth-claim-official.xyz — connect your wallet there before the window closes.
unsolvedCases.rationale: Adversary wraps a drainer lure in protective language. Protective-context suppression should not grant immunity when the same post contains a connect-wallet ask; the conflict should route to review.
unsolvedCases.knownBlindSpot: Protective-context patterns currently suppress the public label without checking whether the speaker adds their own ask.

unsolvedCases.text: Not sure if this is legit but this trading bot literally doubled my USDT in a week 😅 DM me for the link, still can't believe it works. DYOR!
unsolvedCases.rationale: Testimonial-with-doubt is a documented scam format: the doubt markers are camouflage. Should route to review on doubling claim + DM funnel.
unsolvedCases.knownBlindSpot: Doubt markers reduce risk scoring in exactly the way adversaries want.

unsolvedCases.text: Huge thanks to @cryptorecover_pro for getting back my stolen 0.4 BTC after I paid the small processing fee 🙏 DM them if you lost funds, they are the only legit recovery team!
unsolvedCases.rationale: Advance-fee recovery scam advertised as a grateful testimonial — the standard format for re-victimization. The fee mention plus DM funnel should reach review.
unsolvedCases.knownBlindSpot: Testimonial voice ('thanks to...') is not covered by promoter-ask patterns.

unsolvedCases.text: G4N4NCIAS GARANTIZADAS 💸 duplica tu USDT hoy mismo. Envía 50 USDT y recibe 100 de vuelta. Escríbeme al DM, cupos limitados.
unsolvedCases.rationale: Spanish send-to-receive with leetspeak obfuscation of the guarantee keyword. Canonicalization plus Spanish rules should hold the label.
unsolvedCases.knownBlindSpot: Leetspeak canonicalization coverage for non-English keywords is untested beyond this case.

unsolvedCases.text: Trading with my mentor honestly changed my life ❤️ financial freedom is real if you're willing to learn. My DMs are open if you want to know how I started.
unsolvedCases.rationale: Pig-butchering recruitment surface: no explicit return claim, no link, just a DM funnel and a lifestyle promise. The highest-loss typology in IC3 data enters exactly like this.
unsolvedCases.knownBlindSpot: Without an explicit claim or ask, lexical systems have nothing to hold onto. Documented in the threat landscape as the core single-post limitation.

unsolvedCases.text: Everything you need is in the screenshot 👇 send exactly what it shows and you'll get double back within the hour. Don't sleep on this one.
unsolvedCases.rationale: The operative scam content lives in an image the text pipeline cannot see; the doubling promise in text should still carry it to review.
unsolvedCases.knownBlindSpot: OCR evidence extraction is simulated only; image-borne asks are invisible.

unsolvedCases.text: I work at a top-5 exchange (obviously can't say which) and I can get a few people into vetted presales early. Small facilitation fee, serious inquiries only.
unsolvedCases.rationale: Unverifiable insider authority plus an advance fee. No brand name is dropped, so authority-misuse patterns keyed to named brands may not fire.
unsolvedCases.knownBlindSpot: Authority detection is keyed to named entities; anonymous authority claims slip through.

unsolvedCases.text: Avoid fake airdrops out there!! Only use the official verified faucet link in my bio to claim your free 2 ETH safely ✅
unsolvedCases.rationale: Anti-scam framing used to market the poster's own free-claim funnel. 'Free 2 ETH' plus a claim link should force review despite the protective opener.
unsolvedCases.knownBlindSpot: Same suppression gap as unsolved-001, in bait rather than warning form.

unsolvedCases.text: I think I just got scammed by one of those doubling sites... sent 0.2 BTC and got nothing back. I feel so stupid. What do I even do now?
unsolvedCases.rationale: Victim help-seeking must never be labeled. This is a guard case: if it ever fails, the release gate failure is correct and the change that broke it must not ship.

unsolvedCases.text: Week 3 update: staking dashboard showing +11% again 📈 compounding is unreal. Not financial advice but happy to share the platform if anyone's curious.
unsolvedCases.rationale: Serialized fake-platform promotion: each post individually mild, pattern lethal. Single-post review routing on repeated yield claims plus offer-to-share is the best available proxy until account-history enrichment exists.
unsolvedCases.knownBlindSpot: No cross-post memory in the current pipeline; the campaign graph only clusters infrastructure, not serialized behavior by one account.
